steps.title: Fastest path: organize the two area formulas first
steps.content: Start by finding the rectangle's area, then use the given relationship to find the triangle's area. After that, plug the triangle's area and base into the right-triangle area formula.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Area of rectangle X = 32 × 16.5 = 528 mm². The rectangle's area is 3 times the triangle's area, so triangle Y's area = 528 ÷ 3 = 176 mm².

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Area of a right triangle = ½ × base × height. Plug in: 176 = ½ × 22 × h = 11h, so h = 16.
